Here is a comprehensive biography of **Glover Teixeira**, based on his Wikipedia page and other verified sources.

---

## Glover Teixeira — Biography

### Basic Information
| | |
|---|---|
| **Full Name** | Glover Lucas Teixeira |
| **Born** | October 28, 1979 (age 46) |
| **Birthplace** | Sobrália, Minas Gerais, Brazil |
| **Nationality** | Brazilian-American |
| **Height** | 6 ft 2 in (1.88 m) |
| **Weight** | 205 lb (93 kg) |
| **Division** | Light Heavyweight |
| **Reach** | 76 in (193 cm) |
| **Style** | Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu |
| **Years Active** | 2002–2023 |
| **Team** | Teixeira MMA & Fitness (Bethel, Connecticut) |
| **MMA Record** | 33 wins – 9 losses (42 fights total) |

---

### Early Life & Background

Glover Teixeira grew up in the rural community of **Sobrália, Minas Gerais, Brazil**, a place so remote that many homes lacked electricity and the town had only a single gas station. He is of **Portuguese descent**. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

In **1999**, at age 19, Teixeira immigrated to **Danbury, Connecticut** in the United States to help support his family. He found work in landscaping, often working 10 to 12-hour days. It was in Danbury that he met his future wife, **Ingrid**. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

His passion for combat sports was sparked by watching boxing legends like **Mike Tyson** and early MMA fighters — particularly **Royce Gracie** at UFC 1 and UFC 2, and **Chuck Liddell**. Despite having never heard of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu growing up in rural Brazil, those early UFC broadcasts inspired him to pursue a career in mixed martial arts. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

After long landscaping shifts, Teixeira trained in boxing at **Hat City Boxing** and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu at **American Top Team Connecticut**. His talent caught the eye of **John Hackleman**, the legendary trainer of Chuck Liddell, who invited Teixeira to train at **The Pit** in California, where he trained alongside Liddell himself. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

In **November 2020**, Teixeira became a **naturalized American citizen**, stating: *"I'm so proud today because I'm officially an American Citizen. Now I'm very proud to represent both Brazil & America."* [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

---

### Martial Arts Credentials

Teixeira held an impressive array of ranks and certifications: [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

- **8th degree black belt in Kajukenbo (Hawaiian Kempo)** under John Hackleman
- **2nd degree black belt in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu** under Luigi Mondelli
- **Vale Tudo black belt** under Marco Ruas
- **CrossFit certified**
- Former member of the **Brazilian National Wrestling Team**

In grappling, he won **gold at the 2009 ADCC South American Championships** (-99 kg) and **silver at the 2011 ADCC South American Championships** (-99 kg). [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

---

### UFC Career

#### Debut & Rise (2012–2014)

Teixeira signed with the UFC in February 2012 and made a spectacular debut at **UFC 146** (May 26, 2012), submitting **Kyle Kingsbury** in under two minutes of the first round. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

He quickly racked up wins over **Quinton "Rampage" Jackson** (unanimous decision, UFC on Fox 6), **James Te Huna** (submission, earned *Submission of the Night*), and **Ryan Bader** (TKO, earned *Knockout of the Night*). This dominant run earned him a title shot against then-champion **Jon Jones** at **UFC 172** (April 26, 2014). Teixeira lost via unanimous decision in a hard-fought battle. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

#### Mid-Career Setbacks & Resurgence (2014–2019)

After the Jones loss, Teixeira suffered additional setbacks including a decision loss to **Phil Davis** at UFC 179 and a devastating knockout loss to **Anthony Johnson** at UFC 202 (2016, knocked out in the opening seconds). However, he also notched impressive wins over **Ovince Saint Preux** (submission, earned *Fight of the Night*), **Rashad Evans** (KO, earned *Performance of the Night*), and **Misha Cirkunov** (TKO). [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

By 2019, Teixeira was on a resurgence, submitting both **Karl Roberson** and **Ion Cuțelaba** (earning *Performance of the Night* for the latter) and winning a split decision over **Nikita Krylov**. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

#### Championship Run & Title Reign (2020–2023)

In 2020, during the COVID-19 pandemic, Teixeira put together a remarkable win streak: [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

- **Anthony Smith** (May 2020) — Won via 5th-round TKO, earning *Performance of the Night*
- **Thiago Santos** (November 2020) — Won via 3rd-round rear-naked choke submission

This earned him a title shot against champion **Jan Błachowicz** at **UFC 267** (October 30, 2021) in Abu Dhabi. At **42 years and 2 days old**, Teixeira submitted Błachowicz via rear-naked choke in the 2nd round to become the **UFC Light Heavyweight Champion**. This made him: [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

- The **oldest first-time champion in UFC history**
- The **oldest fighter to ever win the UFC Light Heavyweight Championship**
- The **second-oldest UFC champion** in history (behind Randy Couture)

#### Title Defenses & Final Fights

Teixeira defended his title against **Jiří Procházka** at **UFC 275** (June 11, 2022) in Singapore. In an epic back-and-forth battle widely regarded as the **2022 Fight of the Year**, Teixeira lost via rear-naked choke submission in the fifth round. The fight earned him a *Fight of the Night* bonus and was recognized as Fight of the Year by virtually every major MMA outlet (ESPN, Sherdog, Combat Press, World MMA Awards, Wrestling Observer Newsletter, among others). [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

After Procházka vacated the title due to injury, Teixeira faced **Jamahal Hill** for the vacant title at **UFC 283** (January 21, 2023) in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. He lost via unanimous decision after being outstruck for five rounds. During the post-fight interview, Teixeira **announced his retirement** from professional MMA. The fight earned him another *Fight of the Night* bonus. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

---

### UFC Records & Achievements

Teixeira holds numerous UFC Light Heavyweight division records: [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

- **Most post-fight bonuses** in LHW division history — **10**
- **Most finishes** in LHW division history — **13**
- **Most submissions** in LHW division history — **7**
- **Most submission attempts** in LHW division history — **16**
- **Most top position time** in LHW division history — **1:12:16**
- **Second most wins** in LHW division history — **16**
- **Second most total strikes landed** in LHW division history — **1,609**
- **Third most bouts** in LHW division history — **23**
- **Third most total fight time** in LHW division history — **4:25:06**
- **Third most control time** in LHW division history — **1:18:33**
- **Third most significant strikes landed** in LHW division history — **953**
- **Fifth most takedowns landed** in LHW division history — **36**

His 10 post-fight bonuses broke down as: four *Performance of the Night*, one *Knockout of the Night*, one *Submission of the Night*, and four *Fight of the Night* awards. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

---

### Activism & Philanthropy

Throughout his career, Teixeira was active in various causes: [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

- **2013**: Testified before the Connecticut General Assembly to advocate for the legalization and regulation of MMA in Connecticut
- **2013**: Participated in **UFC: Fight for the Troops 3**, supporting the Intrepid Fallen Heroes Fund for wounded military personnel
- **2014**: Met with U.S. Senators **John McCain** and **Harry Reid** at the U.S. Capitol to support brain health research at the Cleveland Clinic's Lou Ruvo Center
- **2014**: Participated in the "Pink Bra" tour for breast cancer awareness and early detection
- **2016**: Received the **American Dream Leadership Award** from the New American Dream Foundation
- **2022**: Visited **The Cure 4 The Kids Foundation**, providing toys and inspiration to children during the holiday season

---

### Post-Retirement

Since retiring, Teixeira has served as a cornerman and coach, most notably working with fellow Brazilian **Alex Pereira**, a former UFC Middleweight and Light Heavyweight champion. He continues to operate his gym, **Teixeira MMA & Fitness**, in Bethel, Connecticut. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira, IMDb]

In June 2023, Teixeira returned to competitive grappling, defeating **Anthony Smith** by decision at UFC Fight Pass Invitational 4. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

He was also awarded the **Key to the City of Danbury** on November 16, 2021, in recognition of his championship achievement and community contributions. [来源: Wikipedia → Glover Teixeira]

---

### Legacy

Glover Teixeira's story is one of perseverance: from a rural Brazilian town without electricity, to landscaping in Connecticut, to becoming a UFC world champion at age 42. His career — spanning over two decades — is defined by his finishing ability (28 of his 33 wins came by knockout or submission), his durability, and his remarkable late-career resurgence. He is widely regarded as one of the most respected and beloved fighters in UFC history.

He holds numerous UFC Light Heavyweight records including most finishes (13), most submissions (7), and most post-fight bonuses (10). Now coaches and runs Teixeira MMA & Fitness in Bethel, Connecticut.

## Biography of Sajid Nadiadwala

**Sajid Nadiadwala** (born 18 February 1966, Mumbai, Maharashtra, India) is an Indian film producer, director, and screenwriter who works in Hindi cinema. He is the owner of **Nadiadwala Grandson Entertainment** (NGE), one of Bollywood's most prolific production houses. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

---

### Early Life & Family

Sajid hails from a prominent filmmaking family. His grandfather, **Abdul Karim Nadiadwala**, moved to Mumbai from Nadiad, Gujarat in 1955 and produced hundreds of films, including *Taj Mahal*. After completing his undergraduate degree, Sajid worked as an assistant director under **J.P. Dutta** on the sets of *Ghulami* at age 21. He is the cousin of fellow producer **Firoz Nadiadwala**. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

---

### Career

#### Founding NGE & Early Years (1992–1995)
At age 25, Sajid established **Nadiadwala Grandson Entertainment Pvt Ltd**. His debut production was *Zulm Ki Hukumat* (1992) starring Dharmendra and Govinda. He followed it with *Waqt Hamara Hai* (1993), which brought together **Akshay Kumar and Suniel Shetty** on screen for the first time. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

#### Breakthrough Hits (1996–2006)
- **Jeet** (1996) – Salman Khan and Sunny Deol starrer; a major hit.
- **Judwaa** (1997) – Salman Khan in a double role; became a cult classic.
- **Har Dil Jo Pyar Karega** (2000) – Salman Khan, Rani Mukherjee, Preity Zinta.
- **Mujhse Shaadi Karogi** (2004) – Akshay Kumar, Priyanka Chopra, Salman Khan.
- **Jaan-E-Mann** (2006) – Commercially unsuccessful. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

#### Expansion & Franchise Building (2007–2014)
- **Heyy Babyy** (2007) – Launched director Sajid Khan.
- **Kambakkht Ishq** (2009) – First Bollywood film to cast Hollywood stars (Sylvester Stallone, Denise Richards, Brandon Routh).
- **Housefull** (2010) – Launched the blockbuster comedy franchise starring Akshay Kumar, Deepika Padukone, and others.
- **Housefull 2** (2012) – Entered the ₹100-crore club, NGE's first.
- **Highway** (2014) – Alia Bhatt and Randeep Hooda, directed by Imtiaz Ali; critically acclaimed.
- **2 States** (2014) – Alia Bhatt and Arjun Kapoor; another ₹100-crore hit. Nominated for Filmfare Best Film.
- **Heropanti** (2014) – Launched Tiger Shroff and Kriti Sanon.
- **Kick** (2014) – Sajid's **directorial debut**, starring Salman Khan and Jacqueline Fernandez. One of the top 100 highest-grossing Indian films. Won him the **IIFA Award for Best Debut Director**. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

#### Continued Dominance (2015–2019)
- **Phantom** (2015), **Tamasha** (2015) – Grossed ₹100 crore worldwide.
- **Baaghi** (2016) – Tiger Shroff and Shraddha Kapoor; grossed ₹129 crore worldwide.
- **Housefull 3** (2016) – Grossed ₹195 crore worldwide.
- **Dishoom** (2016)
- **Judwaa 2** (2017) – Varun Dhawan; earned ₹223 crore worldwide.
- **Baaghi 2** (2018) – Tiger Shroff and Disha Patani; earned ₹258 crore worldwide.
- **Chhichhore** (2019) – Won the **National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi**. Also nominated for Filmfare Best Film. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

#### Recent Work (2020–2026)
Notable productions include *Baaghi 3* (2020), *Tadap* (2021), *83* (2021), *Bachchhan Paandey* (2022), *Satyaprem Ki Katha* (2023), *Bawaal* (2023), *Chandu Champion* (2024), *Sikandar* (2025) starring Salman Khan, *Housefull 5* (2025), *Baaghi 4* (2025), and the upcoming *O'Romeo* (2026). [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

---

### Personal Life

Sajid married actress **Divya Bharti** on 10 May 1992 in a private Nikah ceremony. Tragically, she died on 5 April 1993 after accidentally falling from the balcony of her 5th-floor apartment in Mumbai. He later married journalist **Warda Khan** on 18 November 2000, and they have two sons together. He is known to be close friends with Salman Khan, Akshay Kumar, and Tiger Shroff. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

---

### Awards

| Year | Award | Film | Result |
|------|-------|------|--------|
| 2015 | IIFA Award for Best Debut Director | *Kick* | **Won** |
| 2015 | Filmfare Best Film Award | *2 States* | Nominated |
| 2020 | Filmfare Best Film Award | *Chhichhore* | Nominated |
| 2020 | National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi | *Chhichhore* | **Won** |

[数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

---

### Legacy

With over 50 films produced across three decades, Sajid Nadiadwala has built Nadiadwala Grandson Entertainment into one of Bollywood's most reliable production houses. He is credited with launching the careers of Tiger Shroff and Kriti Sanon (*Heropanti*), resurrecting Jacqueline Fernandez's Bollywood career, and creating enduring franchises like *Housefull*, *Baaghi*, and *Judwaa*. His ability to balance mass-entertainment blockbusters with critically acclaimed films like *Highway*, *Tamasha*, and *Chhichhore* has made him one of the most versatile producers in Hindi cinema. [数据来源: Wikipedia – Sajid Nadiadwala]

# Adil Rami — Biography

**Adil Rami** (Arabic: عَادِل رَامِي; born 27 December 1985) is a French former professional footballer who played as a centre-back. He is best known for his time at Lille, Valencia, AC Milan, Sevilla, and Marseille, as well as being a member of the **France squad that won the 2018 FIFA World Cup**. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami]

---

## Early Life

Rami was born in **Bastia, Corsica, France**, to **Moroccan parents**. He holds dual French and Moroccan citizenship. As a youth, his family moved to the mainland, settling in the southern coast city of **Fréjus**, where his mother worked as a member of the city council. Rami is the **third of four children** — he has two sisters and one brother. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Early life]

While pursuing a football career, Rami worked for the city of Fréjus, doing odd jobs such as maintenance and cleanliness work, as his family was concerned about his future prospects. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Early life]

---

## Club Career

### Fréjus (1994–2006)
Rami registered as a youth player for **Étoile Fréjus Saint-Raphaël** (then ES Fréjus) in 1994 at age nine. He played there as an amateur in the **Championnat de France amateur** (fourth division), initially as an offensive midfielder before switching to centre-back after a teammate's injury. He made **58 league appearances** with 0 goals across three seasons. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Club career]

### Lille (2006–2011)
In 2006, Rami jumped three divisions to join **Ligue 1 club Lille**. He made his professional debut on 19 May 2007 against Auxerre and signed his first professional contract (a three-year deal) on 4 June 2007. He was nicknamed **"Shrek"** by teammates and Lille supporters. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Lille]

In January 2011, he signed with Valencia but was loaned back to Lille for the remainder of the season, during which he was a key part of the **Lille team that won the historic league and cup double** (Ligue 1 and Coupe de France) in the **2010–11 season**. His Lille record: **163 league appearances, 10 goals**. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Lille]

### Valencia (2011–2014)
Rami officially joined **Valencia CF** in La Liga for a reported fee of **€6–10 million**. He made **93 league appearances with 8 goals** for the Spanish club. In September 2013, Valencia suspended him after he publicly criticized his coach and teammates in a radio interview, fining him a club-record **€275,000**. [来源: Wikipedia – Adil Rami → Valencia]

### AC Milan (2014–2015)
Rami moved to **AC Milan**, initially on loan (January 2014) before signing permanently for **£3.37 million (€4.25 million)**. He even personally paid **€500,000** to cover the payment difference between Milan's offer and Valencia's demands. His Milan record: **44 total appearances, 4 goals**. ## Biography of Ylona Garcia

**Ylona Jade Garcia** (born February 28, 2002, in Sydney, Australia) is a Filipino-Australian singer, songwriter, and actress known for her R&B and pop music. She is the third of five siblings; her father is a pathology technician and her mother is a nurse and medical technologist. [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

### Breakthrough: Pinoy Big Brother (2015)
Garcia made her breakthrough in 2015 when she auditioned for ABS-CBN's reality TV series **Pinoy Big Brother: 737**. Selected for her singing, acting, dancing abilities and her "confidence and candid remarks," she became a fan favorite. During her time in the house, she composed and recorded two original songs: **"Win the Fight"** (with fellow housemate Jimboy Martin) and **"Magmahal Muli"** (with Bailey May). She finished as **first runner-up**, garnering 42.24% of the public vote. [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

### Early Career in the Philippines (2015–2020)
Following PBB, Garcia was cast as **Audrey Olivar** in the ABS-CBN romantic comedy series *On the Wings of Love* (2015–2016). In 2016, she signed with **Star Music** and released her debut EP, **"My Name Is Ylona Garcia"** (August 5, 2016), which was certified **Gold** in the Philippines. She also served as a Celebrity VJ for Myx, competed in *We Love OPM: The Celebrity Sing-Offs* (as part of Team Yeng's Oh My Girls, finishing as runners-up), and became a brand ambassador for Coca-Cola Philippines. In 2018, she joined the teen pop group **ASAP G!**, and in 2019 she transferred to the **Homeworkz** label. [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

### International Career with 88rising (2021–present)
In February 2021, Garcia was officially signed to **88rising** through its sub-label **Paradise Rising** (a partnership with Globe Telecom focused on Filipino artists). Her single **"All That"** (March 2021) surpassed one million YouTube views in four days. She followed up with **"Don't Go Changing"** for the *Head in the Clouds* album and delivered a 20-minute spotlight performance at the **Head in the Clouds Festival 2021** at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California. [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

In January 2022, she released **"Entertain Me"** in partnership with the tactical shooter game **VALORANT** by Riot Games. The song was featured in the cinematic trailer for the game's Filipino agent, **Neon**. That same month, she announced she had **relocated to Los Angeles** to pursue her international music career. In August 2022, she became the **first Filipino** to be featured in American fashion magazine **V**, described as "the new global voice leading R&B Pop." [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

### Recent Years (2024–2026)
In August 2024, Garcia joined gaming and lifestyle brand **100 Thieves** as a content creator. She also collaborated with streamers **Valkyrae** and **Fuslie** on the single **"Echoes"** (2024). On February 28, 2025, she released the soft rock single **"Sick of It"**, which she wrote in November 2023 about growing up in a "toxic" entertainment industry. On January 16, 2026, she released two singles: **"Question Mark"** and **"Could've Been."** In June 2025, the *Philippine Daily Inquirer* described her as "more than just a pop star," calling her a "style icon" and "voice of her generation." [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

### Notable Brand Endorsements
Throughout her career, Garcia has endorsed major brands including Coca-Cola, Huawei, Jollibee, Bench, Pond's, Sunsilk, Fujifilm Instax, Mountain Dew, Clean & Clear, Café Amazon, T&J Salon Professionals, and Happy Skin X Disney. [来源: Wikipedia – "Ylona Garcia"]

# Jack Leach — Biography

## Basic Information
**Full name:** Matthew Jack Leach

## Early Life and Education
Jack Leach was born in Taunton, Somerset, and attended Trinity School, Bishop Fox's School, and Richard Huish College, all in Taunton. As a teenager, he worked parking trolleys at a Sainsbury's supermarket in Taunton. At the age of 14, he was diagnosed with **Crohn's disease**, a condition he has managed throughout his professional career. He later completed a degree in sports coaching at Cardiff Metropolitan University (formerly the University of Wales Institute, Cardiff). [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

## Domestic Career — Somerset
Leach graduated from the **Somerset County Cricket Club academy** in the summer of 2010 and signed a professional contract with the club. Before breaking into Somerset's first team, he represented **Dorset County Cricket Club** in the 2010 Minor Counties Cricket Championship, bowling them to victory in the final against Lincolnshire with figures of 6/21 in the second innings. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

While at university, he played for **Cardiff MCCU** in 2011 and 2012. He made his first-class debut for Cardiff MCCU against Somerset in March 2012, bowling 41 overs without taking a wicket as Somerset piled up 642/3 declared. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

He made his Somerset debut in July 2012 in a two-day match against the touring South Africans, notably dismissing **Hashim Amla**. His first-class debut for Somerset came in August 2012 against Lancashire in the County Championship, where he claimed Kyle Hogg as his maiden first-class wicket. He finished the 2012 season ninth in the national first-class averages with 12 wickets at 18.83 runs apiece. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

He spent the 2012–13 winter playing grade cricket for Valley District Cricket Club in Brisbane, Australia. Over the following years, Leach established himself as Somerset's primary spinner and became one of the most consistent slow bowlers in county cricket. As of April 2026, his first-class record stands at **551 wickets in 168 matches** at an average of 27.76, with best figures of 8/85 and 34 five-wicket hauls. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach, ESPNcricinfo stats]

## International Career

### 2018–2021: Sporadic Appearances
Leach made his **Test debut** for England on **30 March 2018** against New Zealand (cap number 684). His early international career was marked by sporadic appearances, often in and out of the side as England rotated their spin options. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

A memorable moment came during the 2019 Lord's Test against Ireland, where Leach — a number 11 batsman — played a remarkable innings, contributing to a famous England victory. His batting performance was so surprising that even his own father, Simon Leach, had not come to watch, never having seen his son bat. [Source: ESPN — "Jack Leach's batting heroics take Ireland – and his own father – by surprise"]

### 2021–Present: First-Choice Spinner
Leach became England's **first-choice spinner** under the Brendon McCullum–Ben Stokes era (dubbed "Bazball"), starting from 2021. Key performances include:

- **India tour 2021:** Took four wickets in England's 227-run victory in the first Test at Chennai in February 2021. [Source: IMDb News/IANS]
- **Sri Lanka tour 2021:** Played in the series in Galle, though he admitted he "came up a little short" during Sri Lanka's second-innings fightback. [Source: Sports Mole]
- **New Zealand tour 2023:** Took 3/45 alongside James Anderson (3/37) as England dominated New Zealand in the second Test at Wellington in February 2023. [Source: IMDb News/IANS]

His last Test appearance (as of the Wikipedia article date) was on **24 October 2024 against Pakistan**. [Source: Wikipedia — Jack Leach]

## Personal Life
Leach has been open about living with **Crohn's disease**, which requires ongoing management. He has become an inspirational figure for managing a chronic health condition while performing at the highest level of international sport.

## Eric Lynn Hacker — Baseball Player (born 1983)

Eric Lynn Hacker (born March 26, 1983, in Duncanville, Texas) is an American former professional baseball pitcher. Standing 6'1" and weighing 230 lbs, he was a switch-hitter who threw right-handed.

**Early Life:** Hacker attended Duncanville High School in Texas. He was the starting quarterback on the football team his senior year but broke his foot in the second game, which led him to concentrate on baseball. He was named a third-team Class 5A All-State pitcher as a senior.

**MLB Career:** Drafted by the New York Yankees in the 23rd round (696th overall) of the 2002 MLB Draft, Hacker spent years in the minor leagues, overcoming Tommy John surgery (2004) and shoulder surgery (2006). He made his MLB debut on September 22, 2009, with the **Pittsburgh Pirates**. He also appeared in the majors with the **Minnesota Twins** (2011) and **San Francisco Giants** (2012). His MLB career totals: 0–1 win–loss record, 4.00 ERA, 11 strikeouts.

**KBO League Career:** Hacker found his greatest success in South Korea's KBO League. He pitched for the **NC Dinos** (2013–2017) in their inaugural season and beyond, and the **Nexen Heroes** (2018). His best season was **2015**, when he led the KBO with **19 wins** alongside a 3.13 ERA and 164 strikeouts, earning the KBO Golden Glove Award for pitchers. Over six KBO seasons, he compiled a **61–37 record** (.622 winning percentage) with a **3.66 ERA** and **675 strikeouts**.

**Pitching Style:** Hacker threw four pitches: fastball, slider, curveball, and changeup.

**Personal Life:** He lives in Southlake, Texas, with his wife Christine (married November 2008). He is a Dallas Cowboys fan and enjoys fishing and pool.

*Source: Wikipedia — "Eric Hacker"*

---

## Eric Nathaniel Hacker — Para-Athlete (born c. 2001)

Eric Nathaniel Hacker (born c. 2001, in Columbus, Ohio; raised in Chillicothe, Ohio) is an American para-athlete who competes in middle- and long-distance running in the **T38 classification** (cerebral palsy).

**Early Life:** Diagnosed with cerebral palsy as an infant, Hacker wore leg braces during early childhood and underwent physical therapy. Despite this, he began competitive running in junior high and became one of the top runners in his area.

**High School:** At Unioto High School (graduated 2020), Hacker qualified 14 times for the OHSAA State Championships in cross-country and track, won multiple conference titles, and helped lead Unioto to a runner-up team finish at the 2019 OHSAA Division II State Cross Country Championships. He received the 2020 Archie Griffin Award for athletic and academic excellence.

**Para-Athletics Achievements:** At the 2019 Adaptive Sports USA Junior Nationals, he won **national titles** in both the 1500 m (4:28.99) and 3000 m T38 events, setting national records in both. He is a **13-time national qualifier**, **two-time All-American**, and **two-time Junior National Champion**. His 1500 m performance (4:29.0) is listed as an American record by Adaptive Track & Field USA. He qualified for the U.S. Paralympic Trials on the pathway toward the Tokyo Paralympic Games.

**College:** Hacker competed for **Shawnee State University** in cross-country and distance track events, earning President's List recognition in 2022.

**Recognition:** In 2019, the Ohio House of Representatives adopted **House Resolution 225** honoring Hacker for his national titles. He has been profiled in *Runner's World* and the USA TODAY Network.

He currently resides in Columbus, Ohio, and continues to train and compete.

*Source: Wikipedia — "Eric Hacker (para-athlete)"*

## Julia Faye — Biography

**Julia Faye Maloney** (September 24, 1892 – April 6, 1966), known professionally as **Julia Faye**, was an American actress of both silent and sound films, best remembered for her long association with legendary director Cecil B. DeMille. Over a career spanning more than four decades (1915–1957), she appeared in more than 30 DeMille productions and over 100 films in total, with roles ranging from maids and ingénues to vamps and queens. [来源: Wikipedia – Julia Faye]

---

### Early Life

Faye was born at her grandmother's home near **Richmond, Virginia**. Her father, **Robert J. Maloney** (born c. 1865), worked for the Atchison, Topeka and Santa Fe Railway, and her mother was **Emma Louise Elliott** (1872–1955), originally from New Castle, Indiana. Her paternal grandfather, Thomas Maloney, was an Irish immigrant who came to the United States in the 1850s. [来源: Wikipedia – Early life]

After her father's death (before 1901), her mother remarried **Cyrus Demetrios Covell** (1862–1941). Faye took her stepfather's surname and was raised in **St. Louis, Missouri**, attending both public and private schools. She studied for a year at **Illinois State University** but dreamed of becoming an actress. [来源: Wikipedia – Early life]

In 1915, while visiting friends in Hollywood, she toured a film studio and was introduced to actor-director **Christy Cabanne**. The two discovered they had been neighbors in St. Louis, and Cabanne persuaded Faye's reluctant mother to let her enter motion pictures. [来源: Wikipedia – Early life]

---

### Career

#### Early Films (1915–1916)

Faye made her debut with bit roles in *Martyrs of the Alamo* and *The Lamb* (both 1915), directed by Cabanne for Triangle Film Corporation. Her first credited role was as **Dorothea** opposite DeWolf Hopper's Don Quixote in the 1915 Fine Arts adaptation. She also appeared in several **Keystone comedies** (1916) and had a minor role in D. W. Griffith's *Intolerance* (1916). [来源: Wikipedia – Triangle, Fine Arts, and Keystone]

#### Cecil B. DeMille and Paramount (1917–1925)

Her first film for Cecil B. DeMille was *The Woman God Forgot* (1917). She became a fixture in DeMille's productions, appearing in *The Whispering Chorus*, *Old Wives for New*, *The Squaw Man*, and *Till I Come Back to You* (all 1918). In *Male and Female* (1919), she played Gloria Swanson's maid. [来源: Wikipedia – Famous Players–Lasky]

One of her most famous roles came in **The Ten Commandments** (1923), where she played **The Wife of Pharaoh** in the prologue. She was listed as a member of the **Paramount Stock Company School** alongside stars like Rudolph Valentino, Gloria Swanson, and Wallace Reid. [来源: Wikipedia – Famous Players–Lasky]

#### DeMille Pictures Corporation (1925–1927)

When DeMille left Famous Players–Lasky to head the Cinema Corporation of America, Faye followed him. **The Volga Boatman** (1926) was a turning point — DeMille called it "his greatest achievement in picture making," and Faye's depiction of a "tiger woman" was acclaimed as the most captivating performance of her career to that point. She had previously been known for "silken siren roles" and was "famed throughout Hollywood for her perfect legs." [来源: Wikipedia – DeMille Pictures Corporation]

She played **Martha** in *The King of Kings* (1927), won critical acclaim for the comedy *Turkish Delight* (1927), portrayed **Empress Josephine** in *The Fighting Eagle* (1927), and had the distinction of being the **first actress to portray Velma on-screen** in the 1927 film adaptation of the play *Chicago*. [来源: Wikipedia – DeMille Pictures Corporation]

#### Sound Films (1928–1957)

Faye made her talkie debut in DeMille's first sound film, *Dynamite* (1929), playing Marcia Towne. After a brief retirement in the early 1930s, she returned to films and appeared in **every one of DeMille's films** after *Union Pacific* (1939). By 1948, she celebrated her **33rd anniversary** as a film actress, with her role in *Chicago Deadline* marking her **250th film assignment**. [来源: Wikipedia – Sound films]

*Samson and Delilah* (1949) was the **35th DeMille film** she appeared in, in a prominent supporting role as Delilah's maidservant **Hisham**. She also made an uncredited appearance in *Sunset Boulevard* (1950) on the set of DeMille's *Samson and Delilah*. In DeMille's 1956 remake of *The Ten Commandments*, she played **Elisheba**, Aaron's wife. Her final role was as a dowager in the 1958 remake of *The Buccaneer*, produced by DeMille. [来源: Wikipedia – Sound films]

---

### Personal Life & Relationship with Cecil B. DeMille

Faye married **Harold Leroy Wallick** on August 2, 1913, in Manhattan. He died in 1918. She later married playwright **Walter Anthony Merrill** on October 24, 1935, but they separated after just ten days and divorced in April 1936, citing incompatibility. [来源: Wikipedia – Personal life]

Faye had a decades-long personal and professional relationship with **Cecil B. DeMille**, becoming one of his mistresses after they met in 1917. DeMille told her she would never become a star because she didn't have "the right kind of personality," describing her as the "cute type" or "soubrette." However, he championed her loyalty, writing in his 1959 autobiography: *"Julia Faye is truly one of those to whom the calendar is absolutely meaningless… Calendars become obsolete. Julia doesn't."* [来源: Wikipedia – Relationship with Cecil B. DeMille]

In the mid-1940s, Faye began writing a memoir titled ***Flicker Faces***. Though never published, excerpts have been included in DeMille biographies by Scott Eyman and John Kobal. The manuscript is preserved in **The Cecil B. DeMille Archives at Brigham Young University**. [来源: Wikipedia – Personal life]

---

### Death & Legacy

Julia Faye died of **cancer** at her home in Pacific Palisades, Los Angeles, on **April 6, 1966**, at the age of 73. Her cremated remains rest in the Colonnade at **Hollywood Forever Cemetery**. [来源: Wikipedia – Death]

For her contributions to the American film industry, she was awarded a **star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame** at 6500 Hollywood Boulevard. [来源: Wikipedia – Legacy]

# Biography of Chadwick Boseman

## Chadwick Aaron Boseman (November 29, 1976 – August 28, 2020)

Chadwick Boseman was an American actor, playwright, and cultural icon whose portrayal of Black historical figures and the Marvel superhero Black Panther made him one of the most influential actors of his generation. Over a two-decade career, he received numerous accolades, including a Golden Globe Award, a Screen Actors Guild Award, and a Primetime Emmy Award, along with posthumous nominations for an Academy Award and a BAFTA Award. [Wikipedia: Chadwick Boseman]

---

## Early Life

Chadwick Aaron Boseman was born on November 29, 1976, in Anderson, South Carolina, where he was raised. His mother, Carolyn, was a nurse, and his father, Leroy, worked at a textile factory and managed an upholstery business. [Wikipedia] As a child, Boseman practiced martial arts — training he continued into adulthood — and initially aspired to become an architect. [Wikipedia] DNA testing later revealed that his ancestors included the Jola people from Guinea-Bissau, the Krio and Limba peoples from Sierra Leone, and the Yoruba people from Nigeria. [Wikipedia]

Boseman graduated from **T. L. Hanna High School** in 1995, where he played on the basketball team. In his junior year, after a classmate was shot and killed, he wrote and staged his first play, *Crossroads*, at the school. He also competed in Speech and Debate, placing eighth in Original Oratory at the 1995 National Tournament. [Wikipedia] Though recruited to play basketball in college, Boseman chose to pursue the arts instead. [Wikipedia]

---

## Education

Boseman attended **Howard University** in Washington, D.C., graduating in 2000 with a **Bachelor of Fine Arts in directing**. [Wikipedia] His teachers included acclaimed actors **Al Freeman Jr.** and **Phylicia Rashad**, who became a mentor. Rashad helped raise funds — notably from her friend **Denzel Washington** — so that Boseman and classmates could attend the **British American Drama Academy's Oxford Summer Program at Balliol College, Oxford**, in England in 1998. [Wikipedia; multiple search results confirming Denzel Washington's support] Boseman initially studied acting to better understand how to relate to actors as a director, but the experience deepened his appreciation for playwriting, particularly the works of Shakespeare, Samuel Beckett, and Harold Pinter. [Wikipedia]

While at Howard, Boseman also traveled to **Ghana** for the first time, working with professor Mike Malone to preserve and celebrate rituals through performance — an experience he called "one of the most significant learning experiences of [his] life." [Wikipedia] After returning to the U.S., he completed additional coursework in film studies at **New York City's Digital Film Academy**. [Wikipedia]

---

## Early Career: Theater and Television (2000–2007)

Boseman settled in Brooklyn, New York, at the start of his career. In 2000, he was named a **Drama League Directing Fellow**, directing productions including George C. Wolfe's *The Colored Museum* and Amiri Baraka's *Dutchman*. [Wikipedia] Between 2002 and 2009, he worked as a drama instructor in the **Schomburg Junior Scholars Program** at the Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture in Harlem. [Wikipedia]

As a playwright and stage actor, Boseman emerged as a prominent voice in the **Hip-Hop theater movement**. His works included *Rhyme Deferred* (co-written with Howard classmate Kamilah Forbes), *Hieroglyphic Graffiti*, and a one-man show called "Red Clay and Carved Concrete." [Wikipedia] He won an **AUDELCO award** in 2002 for his performance in Ron Milner's *Urban Transitions* and was a member of the **National Shakespeare Company of New York**, playing Romeo in *Romeo and Juliet* and Malcolm in *Macbeth*. [Wikipedia]

His best-known play, **Deep Azure** — a work about police brutality largely delivered in rhyme — was commissioned in 2004 by the Congo Square Theatre Company in Chicago and was nominated for a **2006 Jeff Award for Best New Work**. [Wikipedia]

On television, Boseman landed his first role in a 2003 episode of *Third Watch* and was cast as Reggie Montgomery in the soap opera *All My Children*. However, he was **fired after voicing concerns about racist stereotypes** in the script. The role was subsequently recast with his future *Black Panther* co-star **Michael B. Jordan**. [Wikipedia] Boseman later reflected on this experience in his 2018 Howard University commencement address, saying it "seemed to be wrapped up in assumptions about us as black folks." [Wikipedia]

---

## Film Breakthrough (2008–2015)

Boseman moved to **Los Angeles** in 2008 to pursue film acting. [Wikipedia] His first feature film role was as football player Floyd Little in *The Express: The Ernie Davis Story* (2008). He also had a recurring role on the TV series *Lincoln Heights* and appeared as a series regular on the NBC drama *Persons Unknown* (2010). [Wikipedia]

### *42* (2013)
Boseman's **breakthrough role** came as baseball legend **Jackie Robinson** in the film *42* (2013). Director Brian Helgeland cast him after being impressed by Boseman's bravery in choosing to read the film's most difficult scene during auditions. [Wikipedia] Boseman trained for five months with professional baseball coaches to replicate Robinson's mannerisms. Robinson's widow, **Rachel Robinson**, said that watching Boseman's performance was "like seeing her husband again." [Wikipedia] Even critics who were lukewarm on the film praised Boseman's performance. [Wikipedia]

### *Get on Up* (2014)
The following year, Boseman portrayed the **Godfather of Soul, James Brown**, in *Get on Up* (2014). He performed all of his own dancing and some singing, training five to eight hours a day for two months. [Wikipedia] His performance was universally acclaimed; *Time*'s Richard Corliss wrote that Boseman "deserves a Pulitzer, a Nobel and instant election to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame." [Wikipedia] Director Tate Taylor had sought Boseman as his only choice for the role. [Wikipedia]

### *Marshall* (2017)
Boseman continued his pattern of portraying iconic Black figures by playing a young **Thurgood Marshall** — years before Marshall became the first African American Supreme Court Justice — in *Marshall* (2017). The film premiered at Howard University, which both Boseman and Marshall had attended. [Wikipedia]

---

## Global Stardom: Black Panther and the MCU (2016–2019)

In 2016, Boseman began portraying **T'Challa / Black Panther** in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, signing a five-picture deal with Marvel. [Wikipedia] He did not audition for the role, instead having a "discussion" with Marvel about their shared vision for the character. [Wikipedia]

His first appearance was in **Captain America: Civil War** (2016), where his performance was highly praised. Boseman learned **Xhosa** from co-star John Kani (who played his father) and insisted on using the language for the character, developing a distinct **Wakandan accent** that he used throughout production whether on camera or not. [Wikipedia]

### *Black Panther* (2018)
The standalone **Black Panther** (2018), directed by **Ryan Coogler**, was a landmark in cinema — the first mega-budget superhero film with a predominantly Black cast and director, and the first superhero film nominated for the **Academy Award for Best Picture**. [Wikipedia] Boseman was named to the **2018 Time 100** list of the world's most influential people. [Wikipedia] Critics praised his performance for its "sub-Shakespearean gravitas" and "quiet dignity." [Wikipedia]

He reprised the role in **Avengers: Infinity War** (2018) and **Avengers: Endgame** (2019), both of which were the highest-grossing films of their respective release years, with *Endgame* becoming the highest-grossing film of all time at that point. [Wikipedia]

---

## Illness, Death, and Final Performance

In 2016, Boseman was diagnosed with **colon cancer**. He kept his condition entirely private, continuing to work and deliver powerful performances while undergoing treatment. [Wikipedia]

Boseman died on **August 28, 2020**, at the age of **43**, at his home in Los Angeles. [Wikipedia] He was buried in his hometown of Anderson, South Carolina. [Wikipedia] His death shocked the world, as even close colleagues were unaware of his illness. [Search results]

His final film role was as trumpeter **Levee Green** in **Ma Rainey's Black Bottom** (2020), an adaptation of August Wilson's play directed by George C. Wolfe — the same Wolfe whose work Boseman had directed early in his career. [Wikipedia] For this performance, Boseman received posthumous **Golden Globe** and **Screen Actors Guild Awards** for Best Actor, along with a posthumous **Academy Award nomination**. [Wikipedia]

He also posthumously won a **Primetime Emmy Award** for Outstanding Character Voice-Over Performance for his final portrayal of T'Challa in the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* (2021). [IMDb]

Boseman was married to singer **Taylor Simone Ledward**. [Wikipedia]

---

## Legacy and Honors

- **Howard University** renamed its College of Fine Arts the **Chadwick A. Boseman College of Fine Arts** in his honor. [IMDb]
- He received a posthumous **Hollywood Walk of Fame star** approximately five years after his death, permanently commemorating his contributions to the film industry. [Search result, 2025]
- At the 2019 SAG Awards, accepting the award for Outstanding Performance by a Cast for *Black Panther*, Boseman delivered a powerful speech: "We know what it's like to be told there isn't a screen for you to be featured on, a stage for you to be featured on... We knew that we could create a world that exemplified a world we wanted to see." [Wikipedia]

Chadwick Boseman's legacy endures not only through his groundbreaking performances but through the barriers he broke as a Black leading man in Hollywood, the historical figures he brought to life with dignity and power, and the quiet courage with which he faced his own mortality while continuing to create art that inspired millions.
